category: Brew-recipe dose mistaken for retail bag size

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster name/location, origin, region, process, varieties, producer, altitude, harvest, tasting notes, filter roast designation, default 200g size, USD 22.00 one-time price, package grams, and availability are explicit. Boolean inferences are also supported: single-origin Ethiopia implies not a blend; 'Roasted for: Filter' supports not espresso; no decaf or coferment/infusion is indicated.

realism.reasons: This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee roaster product page. The structure is very natural: header/nav, product title, price, size selector, purchase options, sensory notes, production details, brew guidance, descriptive copy, cross-sell, variant block, and footer. The voice matches contemporary specialty coffee ecommerce well—concise, confident, and origin-focused without sounding overblown. The origin data is coherent and realistic: Chelbesa/Danche in Gedeo, Yirgacheffe, washed process, JARC 74110/74112, and 1,950–2,200 masl all fit known Ethiopian coffee patterns. The harvest window is believable, and the tasting notes align with a washed high-elevation Yirgacheffe profile. The brewing recipe and the note distinguishing brew dose from bag size feel especially authentic, since that kind of clarification often appears when product pages include recipe text near variant selectors. The variant data block is also convincing: sensible SKUs, separate subscription variant, proper compare-at price for the subscription discount, and package weights that map cleanly to the size options. Minor tiny tells only: the explicit label '[default_for_inference]' is not something a shopper-facing page would show, and the standalone embedded variant section is more like extracted backend/product JSON than pure rendered page text. But as an html-to-text rendering that includes scraped structured product data, it is entirely believable.

label.coffee.sensory_text: Tasting notes: jasmine, white peach, lemon curd, black tea
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: jasmine, white peach, lemon curd, black tea
label.coffee.producer_text:
This washed lot comes from Danche, a small washing station near Chelbesa in the Gedeo zone of Yirgacheffe. Local smallholder farmers deliver ripe cherry grown on garden plots at high elevation, where cool nights help preserve the florals we look for in coffees from this area.

After hand sorting, the coffee is depulped, fermented in clean water, washed through channels and dried slowly on raised beds.

coffee.sensory_text: jasmine, white peach, lemon curd, black tea
coffee.display_tasting_notes: jasmine, white peach, lemon curd, black tea
coffee.producer_text: This washed lot comes from Danche, a small washing station near Chelbesa in the Gedeo zone of Yirgacheffe. Local smallholder farmers deliver ripe cherry grown on garden plots at high elevation, where cool nights help preserve the florals we look for in coffees from this area. After hand sorting, the coffee is depulped, fermented in clean water, washed through channels and dried slowly on raised beds. The cup is delicate and precise: jasmine in the aroma, white peach sweetness, a bright lemon-curd acidity and a clean black-tea finish.
